stories.headline: OpenAI files confidential S-1 with SEC, readying Wall Street debut one week after Anthropic
stories.summary: OpenAI submitted a confidential S-1 registration statement to the SEC on June 8, 2026 — one week after rival Anthropic did the same. The company is valued at over $850 billion and generating $2 billion in revenue per month, up from $1 billion per quarter at end-2024. Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are advising on a potential fall listing, though no timing is confirmed. OpenAI noted the filing might leak and proactively announced it.
stories.raw_excerpt: OpenAI said it was generating $2 billion in revenue per month, up from $1 billion per quarter at the end of 2024.

stories.headline: Anthropic files preliminary IPO paperwork with the SEC, joining OpenAI in public-markets race
stories.summary: Anthropic filed a confidential preliminary S-1 with the SEC around June 1, 2026 — a move that appears to have prompted OpenAI to follow suit days later. Both leading AI safety-focused labs filing for IPO within the same week represents a significant shift in how frontier AI is being financed and who it is ultimately accountable to.
stories.raw_excerpt: Anthropic prepares to sell stock to the public; files preliminary IPO paperwork.

stories.headline: Anthropic calls for global AI pause days after its own IPO filing — as Claude writes 80% of its code
stories.summary: On June 4, 2026, Anthropic published 'When AI builds itself,' co-authored by Marina Favaro and co-founder Jack Clark, proposing a coordinated international pause on frontier AI development. The report reveals that over 80% of code merged into Anthropic's own production codebase is now authored by Claude — up from low single digits before February 2025. Anthropic's proposal is conditional: it would only pause if other frontier labs do so under verifiable conditions. The timing, days after Anthropic's own IPO filing, has drawn immediate scrutiny.
stories.raw_excerpt: More than 80 percent of code merged into Anthropic's production codebase was authored by Claude — up from low single digits before February 2025.

stories.headline: Microsoft launches seven in-house AI models at Build 2026, explicitly reducing reliance on OpenAI
stories.summary: At Build 2026 on June 2, Microsoft unveiled seven MAI models including MAI-Thinking-1 (35B active parameters, 256K context) and MAI-Code-1-Flash. The company explicitly framed the launch as reducing dependency on OpenAI and Anthropic. In blind evaluations, MAI-Thinking-1 reportedly outperformed GPT-5.5 at a tenth of the cost. Microsoft also introduced Autopilots — always-on autonomous agents — and positioned Windows as an agent platform.
stories.raw_excerpt: Microsoft can run its models on its own Azure cloud infrastructure and avoid paying third parties such as OpenAI.

stories.headline: Hackers used prompt injection on Meta's AI support chatbot to hijack Instagram accounts, including Obama White House handle
stories.summary: Attackers exploited Meta's AI-powered support chatbot via prompt injection to gain access to Instagram accounts including the Obama-era White House handle and the US Space Force chief master sergeant's account. The exploit worked by asking the bot to add a new email to the target account, which sent a verification code to the attacker. The vulnerability had been active since February 2026 and was reportedly resolved in early June 2026.
stories.raw_excerpt: Compromised accounts included the Instagram handle for the Obama-era White House and the account of the US Space Force's chief master sergeant.

stories.headline: Google launches Android fake call detection using RCS handshake to fight AI deepfake voice scams
stories.summary: Google announced on June 2, 2026, that Android is rolling out a fake call detection feature using end-to-end encrypted RCS technology. When both parties use Phone by Google, a silent device-to-device handshake verifies calls in real time — warning users if the call is not coming from the contact's actual device. INTERPOL cited impersonation fraud as contributing to over $400 billion in global losses, with AI voice cloning making the scams harder to detect.
stories.raw_excerpt: Scammers are spoofing trusted phone numbers and using AI deepfake technology to sound like authority figures, family members, or employers.

stories.headline: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT 'Dreaming V3' — a background memory system that updates itself without user review
stories.summary: OpenAI began rolling out Dreaming V3 on June 4-5, 2026, replacing ChatGPT's manually curated memory list with a background synthesis process that reads across years of past conversations and auto-updates memories without user input. Critics noted the system limits the audit trail users previously had over stored memories. Rollout started with US Plus and Pro subscribers, with Free and international tiers to follow.
stories.raw_excerpt: OpenAI rewrites personalization engine, limits audit trail.

stories.headline: xAI replaces college-aged Grok training lead with SpaceX Starlink veteran; staff asked to justify their work
stories.summary: xAI brought in Jack Garabedian, a Starlink engineer from SpaceX, to replace Diego Pasini as head of the human data team that trains Grok. Bloomberg reported on June 9 that some xAI employees have been asked to explain and justify their own work as part of a broader accountability review. Reports also indicate 9 of xAI's 12 original co-founders have now departed the company.
stories.raw_excerpt: Leadership changes hit the xAI team training Grok, and some employees have been asked to explain their work.

stories.headline: Tech layoffs reach 142,000 in 2026 as profitable companies redirect payroll into $700B AI infrastructure
stories.summary: Tech layoffs in 2026 have surpassed 142,000, with Oracle alone cutting 30,000 positions, even as the four largest hyperscalers — Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta — committed a combined $700 billion in capital expenditure for AI infrastructure. The defining feature is that these cuts are happening at profitable companies: headcount is being converted directly into GPU procurement and data center buildout. AI is cited as the reason for roughly 17% of all 2026 job cuts.
stories.raw_excerpt: Tech layoffs in 2026 have hit 142,000 as profitable companies including Meta, Amazon, and Oracle cut jobs to fund a combined $700 billion AI infrastructure buildout.

stories.headline: White House issues AI executive order framed as deregulation, which critics say centralises federal control
stories.summary: On June 2, 2026, the White House issued an executive order titled 'Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security,' prioritising national security and industry collaboration. Legal analysts note the order seeks to preempt state-level AI regulation — a Science journal article characterised it as significant federal intervention disguised as deregulation.

stories.headline: Colorado's AI Act gutted before enforcement: xAI wins court stay, Governor signs watered-down replacement
stories.summary: Colorado's comprehensive AI Act, originally due to enforce June 30, 2026, has been dismantled from two directions: Governor Polis signed SB 189 in May 2026, replacing the original risk-based framework with narrow disclosure requirements and delaying enforcement to January 2027; and a federal judge separately stayed the original law after xAI filed for a preliminary injunction. This marks the first time an AI company has successfully used federal courts to block a state AI law.

stories.headline: AI deepfake videos of UK politicians Farage and Bailey spread on X as investment fraud linked to Russian networks
stories.summary: AI-generated videos falsely depicting Nigel Farage and Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey in a violent confrontation were spread on X as part of an investment fraud operation reportedly linked to Russian-language cybercriminal networks. Governor Bailey publicly warned the scams exploit vulnerable users. The incident illustrates how deepfake technology is increasingly weaponised for financial fraud using fabricated authority-figure confrontations.

stories.headline: NVIDIA releases Nemotron 3 Ultra 550B — one of the largest open-weight models from a chip manufacturer
stories.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 Ultra 550B A55B open-weight model on June 4, 2026. The release represents NVIDIA's push to compete in the open-weight model space alongside its GPU hardware business, and is among the largest open-weight models released by a major chip manufacturer to date.

stories.headline: OpenAI releases GPT-Rosalind — a purpose-built model for drug discovery and life sciences enterprise
stories.summary: OpenAI released GPT-Rosalind on June 4, 2026, a model purpose-built for life sciences research at enterprise scale, combining GPT-5.5's agentic coding and tool-use capabilities with domain-specific optimisation for drug-discovery tasks. The model targets pharmaceutical enterprise clients and represents OpenAI's broader push into sector-specific deployments beyond general-purpose use.

stories.headline: MiniMax M3 released as first open-weight model combining frontier coding, 1M context, and native multimodality
stories.summary: Chinese AI company MiniMax released M3 on June 1, 2026, claiming it is the first open-weight model to combine frontier-level coding, a 1-million-token context window, and native multimodality in a single model. It tops the open-weight SWE-Bench Pro at 59.0%. The release challenges the assumption that open-weight models lag significantly behind closed frontier models.

stories.headline: WHO warns AI is entering health policy faster than institutions can govern it
stories.summary: The WHO published a discussion paper on June 2, 2026, on the opportunities and risks of AI in evidence-informed health policy. The paper warns that 'AI is entering health policy work faster than most institutions have built the capacity to govern it' and emphasises that AI should augment — not automate — human judgment in health decisions. It represents the WHO's most detailed AI governance guidance to date.
stories.raw_excerpt: AI is entering health policy work faster than most institutions have built the capacity to govern it.

stories.headline: Anthropic holds Code w/ Claude developer conference, doubles Claude Code rate limits
stories.summary: Anthropic held its annual developer conference, Code w/ Claude, in San Francisco during the week of June 2, 2026. Anthropic doubled rate limits on Claude Code and raised API limits for Claude Opus at the event, signalling investment in developer adoption. The conference coincided with Anthropic's IPO filing and publication of the 'When AI builds itself' report — an unusually packed communications week.

stories.headline: OpenAI grants EU governments and cybersecurity teams limited access to GPT-5.5-Cyber
stories.summary: OpenAI extended EU access to GPT-5.5-Cyber — a variation of its flagship model optimised for cybersecurity applications — in limited preview to vetted cybersecurity teams, EU businesses, governments, cybersecurity authorities, and EU institutions. The move represents OpenAI's effort to expand into regulated European markets and government security use cases.
